reasoning: The document contains a critical programmatic defect regarding pervasive header and footer artifacts across all 6 pages of the output. According to the count-based routing thresholds, any critical defect automatically requires a REJECT decision. Additionally, there is a major issue with mixed numbering types in lists indicating flattened sub-points, and a minor loss of visual emphasis.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (6): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 6; Page 3 of 6
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6, pages_affected=6/6. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source document relies heavily on bold and underlined text to demarcate inline topics within its unordered lists (e.g., 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Dam Safety'). The extraction JSON failed to preserve this emphasis, providing plain text strings without the expected markdown syntax (such as **Air Quality**) or underline preservation.
concerns.location: Page 3 and Page 4, unordered list items

action_items: Remove duplicate header and footer artifacts from all pages
Fix list structures to ensure sub-points are properly nested rather than flattened
Restore bold and underline visual emphasis for inline topics within unordered lists
